title: Metal making and treating process operatives
description: Execute or adapt procedures and techniques for calibrating measurement devices, by applying knowledge of measurement science, mathematics, physics, chemistry, and electronics, sometimes under the direction of engineering staff. Determine measurement standard suitability for calibrating measurement devices. May perform preventive maintenance on equipment. May perform corrective actions to address identified calibration problems. All engineering technologists and technicians, except drafters, not listed separately. Test the safety of structures, vehicles, or vessels using x-ray, ultrasound, fiber optic or related equipment. (Based on 70 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Position patterns inside mold sections, cut spouts, runner holes, and reassemble molds for pouring molten metal.
Rotate sweep boards to create symmetrical molds, and lift upper mold sections to remove molded patterns.
Pour molten metal into molds manually or with crane ladles, and operate ovens or furnaces to bake cores or melt metal.
Sift and pack sand into mold sections, clean and smooth molds, and repair surface imperfections.
Move workpieces using cranes and signal others, mark weld points, and assemble components into holding fixtures to bond or weld them.
Read blueprints and specifications, set dials and controls to regulate processes, and ensure safety in operations.
Inspect, measure, and test completed workpieces to ensure conformance to specifications and record operational information.
Adjust equipment and controls to correct problems or meet specifications, and maintain machines to ensure efficient operation.
Weld components in various positions, clean parts, and prepare surfaces by removing contaminants before bonding.
Select and install torches, tips, rods, and flux based on specifications, and follow safety protocols during operations.
Monitor equipment operation, detect issues and make adjustments as necessary to ensure processes meet specifications.
Set up and tend machines for molding, casting, or extruding processes, and record machine malfunctions.
Perform maintenance work such as cleaning and replacing worn parts, and manage material inventories as necessary.
Mix and measure materials, check dimensions for accuracy, and position workpieces securely in machines prior to processing.
Operate metal shaping and forming machines, adjust settings, and ensure proper functioning throughout the process.
Observe machine operations, control boards, and gauges to detect malfunctions such as clogged bushings and defective components.
Notify other workers of defects, and direct them to adjust extruding and forming machines as necessary.
Inspect, feel, and measure workpieces to ensure that surfaces and dimensions meet specifications.
Monitor machine operations to determine whether adjustments are necessary, stopping machines when problems occur.
Clean and maintain extruding and forming machines, using hand tools and solvents as required.
Examine assembled or finished products to ensure conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments.
Select and acquire metals and gems for designs and compute labor and material costs to determine production costs.
Shape and straighten damaged or twisted articles by hand or using pliers, and soften metal for shaping as needed.
Create new jewelry designs and modify existing designs, utilizing computers and CAD software where necessary.
Record operational data and weights of finished pieces, ensuring accuracy and compliance with specifications.
Build structural components like frames, beams, or girders according to blueprints or directions from supervisors.
Cut, bend, or weld metal pieces using appropriate tools and equipment to fabrication specifications.
Fasten structural steel members to properly align and secure them using bolts as directed by plans.
Assemble, maintain, and verify the operation of machines such as drills, shears, and grinders for metal fabrication.
Debug and maintain testing or mechanical equipment for operational efficiency and compliance with safety standards.
Examine finished products or samples to ensure they meet quality standards and specifications using various techniques.
Drill, cut, or grind materials to specific dimensions as required by the manufacturing process.
Organise and direct tasks for fabrication to ensure efficient workflow and optimal material usage.
Turn dials, handwheels, valves, or switches to regulate conveyor speeds, machine temperature, and air pressure.
Hammer, chip, and grind workpieces to cut, bend, and straighten metal.
Clean equipment and work areas, including removing impurities or excess materials from objects.
Monitor equipment operation, gauges, and panel lights to detect deviations from standards and adjust controls as necessary.
Examine or test samples of processed substances for conformance to specifications, and verify quality of finished workpieces.
Pour and regulate the flow of molten metal into molds and forms to produce ingots or other castings.
Install assemblies or fixtures such as pipes, ducts, or tanks according to blueprints and specifications.
Load specified amounts of metal and flux into furnaces or clay crucibles and perform necessary calculations for processing.
Mix or blend ingredients or chemicals for processing, ensuring proper ratios and specifications.
Repair and maintain metal forms and equipment, making adjustments or modifications when necessary.
Set up, operate, or tend plating or coating machines to coat metal or plastic products with various metals.
Immerse workpieces in coating solutions or liquid metal or plastic for specified times and inspect coated areas for defects.
Weigh or measure materials, ingredients, or products to ensure conformance and collect samples for laboratory testing.
Monitor equipment to ensure safe and efficient operation, and stop mixing or blending machines when specified qualities are obtained.
Operate or tend machines to mix or blend a variety of materials such as chemicals or food products, and clean finished items.
Shape metal materials using hand tools and tend accessory equipment, such as pumps or conveyors, in production processes.
Read work orders to determine production specifications and maintain production records.
Inspect materials and products for defects to verify conformance to specifications, including marking defects for removal.
Collect or select samples for testing or use as models and record inspection or test data such as weights or temperatures.
Operate machinery used in the production process, assist operators, and handle raw materials, finished products, and machine upkeep.
Examine materials, ingredients, or products visually to ensure conformance to established standards, and measure them using instruments.
Transfer materials and products between machinery and equipment, and dislodge jammed materials from machinery.
Discard or reject products, materials, or equipment not meeting specifications.
Study blueprints, drawings, and sketches to determine material dimensions, required equipment, and operational sequences.
Set up and operate machines, such as lathes, drill presses, and milling machines, to fabricate prototypes or model parts.
Inspect and test products using precision measuring instruments or circuit testers to verify conformance to specifications.
Cut, shape, and form metal parts using lathes, saws, power brakes, and hand tools.
Record specifications, production operations, and final dimensions for establishing operating standards and procedures.
Rework or alter component models as required to ensure products meet standards.
Consult with engineering personnel to discuss developmental problems and recommend product modifications.
Align, fit, and join parts using bolts, screws, welding, or gluing methods.
Transfer finished products, materials, and tools between storage and work areas of plants and warehouses.
Monitor equipment to ensure compliance with specifications and make necessary adjustments.
Measure, mark, and scribe metal or plastic stock to lay out machining.
Weld, repair, and fabricate equipment or machinery according to specifications.
Operate machines that harden, anneal, or heat-treat metals, and monitor processes to ensure proper treatment.
Test units for operability before and after repairs to ensure proper functionality.
Replace defective parts, using hand tools and machinery to maintain equipment functionality.
Dismantle machines and equipment for repair and ensure proper reassembly after maintenance work.
Instruct new workers in machine operation and safety procedures.
Adjust controls to maintain temperatures and heating times, using thermal instruments, charts, dials, and gauges of furnaces.
Determine flame temperatures, current frequencies, heating cycles, and induction heating coils needed based on stock properties.
Load parts into containers and place containers on conveyors or directly insert parts into furnaces.
Inspect or test damaged machine parts and mark defective areas or advise supervisors of repair needs.
Start machines and observe mechanical operation to determine efficiency and detect problems.
Repair and replace damaged or worn parts, and perform minor maintenance like lubrication or cleaning on machines.
Test mechanical products and equipment after repair to ensure performance and compliance with specifications.
Operate and inspect machines or heavy equipment to diagnose defects and ensure proper functionality.
Load stock, start conveyors, and open furnace doors, or signal crane operators during the heating process.
Clean machines and parts using cleaning solvents, abrasives, and other equipment to maintain operational efficiency.
Collect turbine data for testing or research and inspect machines for defects using precision instruments.
Dismantle machinery to remove parts for repair or maintenance, and reassemble after repairs.
Set up and operate machines for precision machining operations, ensuring alignment and securing of stock material.
Measure, examine, or test completed units to check for defects using precision measuring instruments, ensuring conformance to specifications.
Read work orders and specifications to determine machines needing maintenance or repair, and keep service records.
Collaborate with other workers to repair or move machines and maintain inventories of necessary parts and supplies.
Lay out designs or dimensions from sketches or blueprints on surfaces using measuring instruments.
Study artistic objects or graphic materials to plan carving or cutting techniques.
Drill holes and cut or carve moldings and grooves in stone according to patterns.
Shape, trim, or touch up designs with appropriate tools to finish carvings.
Select tools and determine their sequence of use.
Inspect machined parts to verify conformance to specifications.
Operate and adjust controls of heating equipment to melt material or cure molds.
Move and position workpieces using hoists, clamps, and securing devices.
Plan operations and sequences for machining processes, using measuring devices.
Design and prepare templates for various processes.
Measure and inspect products to ensure conformance to specifications.
Operate and maintain machinery for grinding, drilling, and finishing.
Observe machine operations to detect problems, making necessary adjustments.
Report, repair, or replace machine parts and notify personnel of issues.
Set up and operate machines to process metal or plastic material.
Verify accuracy of shapes and sizes using measuring instruments.
Clean work area regularly and maintain supplies of tools and materials.
Study blueprints, layouts, or charts to determine specifications, tooling instructions, material requirements, and operational sequences.
Lift metal stock or workpieces manually or using hoists, and position and secure them in machines using tools and fasteners.
Crank machines through cycles, adjusting tool positions and machine controls to ensure specified timing, clearances, and tolerances.
Select cutting tools and set cutting specifications, speeds, feed rates, and depths of cuts according to written specifications or knowledge of metal properties.
Start machines, monitor operations, and adjust controls to ensure that products conform to specifications and to detect any malfunction.
Adjust machine controls and change tool settings or replace worn cutting tools to keep dimensions within specified tolerances.
Verify workpiece alignment and specifications using measuring instruments such as calipers, micrometers, and gauges.
Install and secure tools and accessories on machines, using hand tools or power tools, following specifications.
Observe machine operations to detect problems, perform minor repairs, and conduct routine maintenance on equipment.
Chip slag from ladles or remove linings when beyond repair, using hammers and chisels, to maintain equipment.
Press, shape, or cut materials to specifications using machinery, adjusting controls as necessary during the process.
Record production output and document any issues related to machinery or workpiece specifications.
Confer with supervisors or other workers to resolve production issues or obtain approval to make adjustments to machine operations.
Clean work areas, tools, and machines to maintain a safe and organized work environment.
Analyze job orders, blueprints, specifications, and design data to calculate dimensions, tool selection, and machine settings.
Observe machines during trial runs or conduct simulations to ensure machinery functions properly and meets specifications.
Write and modify programs for computer-controlled machines, ensuring proper machine operations and efficiency.
Input and enter coordinates and dimensions into machine control panels and perform preventive maintenance on machines.
Sort shop orders to maximize material utilization and minimize machine setup time.
Set up and operate computer-controlled machines or robots on metal or plastic workpieces, adjusting controls as needed.
Visually inspect equipment for defects and ensure proper maintenance and lubrication during machine operations.
Conduct calibration tests and maintain or repair measurement devices used for calibration testing.
Document calibration procedures and results, and develop new calibration methods or techniques.
Perform non-destructive testing to identify defects in materials using various techniques and record the results.
Monitor inventory levels and order supplies and materials needed for operations.
Evaluate and implement maintenance policies and procedures, and coordinate training for employees in safety and maintenance techniques.
